hypothesis: A homotopy-based refinement that begins by optimizing against artificially broadened pseudo-Voigt peaks (e.g., scaling the Caglioti parameters U,V,W by 3–5× and increasing η toward Gaussian) and monotonically anneals to the physical broadening will smooth the XRD-similarity loss enough to eliminate most spurious local minima and, on the MP20-derived test set with σl=0.05–0.10 or σc=0.02–0.05, will achieve at least 2× higher StructureMatcher match rates than direct fixed-broadening optimization using the same similarity metric, without imposing symmetry constraints.
